authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit / Eldorado National Forest (Desolation Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Grass Lake not listed)

CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Grass Lake not listed; only 'Tahoe Lake and tributaries' (152) special listing appears, and it reaches flowing tributaries upstream to the first lake — not this lake)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Grass Lake not listed among El Dorado Co. release waters)
Desolation Wilderness: Cherished and Guarded — Tahoe Quarterly
sources.quotedText: Plans are under way this summer to use gillnetting and electrofishing in the same lakes that were previously treated—Tamarack, Ralston, Cagwin, Lake Margery and Lake Lucille, plus Le Conte and Jabu, according to U.S. Forest Service reports. ... fish still populate most of Desolation's lakes, including rainbow, brown, brook—the most common catch—and golden trout, and even mackinaw in a few waters.

sources.title: Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Wilderness Permits — USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

sources.title: Eldorado National Forest — Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking — USDA Forest Service
sources.quotedText: Day users may obtain a free Day Use permit from the Placerville Ranger Station or may issue themselves a day use permit at most major trailheads in the summer. During the winter, day use permits are not required (roughly October to March depending on weather conditions).

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 260785, "Grass Lake", Lake, El Dorado County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":260785,"gaz_name":"Grass Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Lake","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13370881.0902, 4703457.5733) converts to lon -120.112668, lat 38.872730, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-120.1132607, 38.8725924) to within ~55m.

parameters.note: Grass Lake is a subalpine lake in the Desolation Wilderness (El Dorado Co.) on the Glen Alpine Trail, within the Lake Tahoe basin — its outlet, Glen Alpine Creek, flows to Fallen Leaf Lake and thence via Taylor Creek to Lake Tahoe. It is a standing lake, not a flowing tributary, so the special Lake Tahoe tributary rule (§7.50(b)(152), which reaches only 'tributaries upstream to the first lake' and the lake within 300 ft of their mouths) does not apply to the lake itself. The lake is not listed by name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1): open all year, 5/10. Unlike neighboring Le Conte and Jabu lakes, Grass Lake is not among the lakes treated under the USFS frog-restoration fish-removal project, and angler reports describe a self-sustaining wild trout fishery (brook/brown); however, CDFW's 2026 provisional plan does not list it for stocking and no agency source names a current species, so the species list is left empty rather than asserting fish from unofficial reports.

parameters.description: Grass Lake lies inside the Desolation Wilderness (USDA Forest Service, Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit / Eldorado National Forest), reached via the Glen Alpine Trail. A wilderness permit is required to enter: overnight use requires a permit year-round (with a quota system from the Friday before Memorial Day through Sept. 30, reserved via Recreation.gov), and day-use permits are required in summer (free, self-issued at major trailheads); day-use permits are not required in winter (roughly October–March).

raw.day_use_permit: required in summer (free, self-issue at trailheads); not required in winter
